title.en: Right on! That's it! 맞아! 바로 그거야!

description.en: When you totally agree with someone or they got something right! Practice responding with "Maja" and "Baro geugeoya".
description.ko: 상대방의 말에 전적으로 동의하거나. 무언가 정답을 맞혔을 때! "맞아"와 "바로 그거야"를 써서 시원하게 호응해 봅니다.

content.speaker: John
content.text: What's up world. Welcome back to Talkori. I'm your host. John.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 안녕하세요 여러분~! 오늘도 여러분의 한국어 실력을 쑥쑥 키워드릴 미나입니다.
content.translation: Hello everyone~! I'm Mina. here to boost your Korean skills again today.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Mina. I've been practicing that difficult Korean tongue twister. Listen! "간장공장 공장장은..."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 오~ 간장공장 공장장은..., 과연?
content.translation: Oh~ 'The factory manager of the soy sauce factory...,' let's see?

content.speaker: John
content.text: 강공장장이고!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 와~! 맞아~! 바로 그거예요!
content.translation: Wow~! 'Right~!' That's exactly it!

insight.title: 맞아
insight.usage_tip: The most common way to agree with someone.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "좋았어~!" 존 씨. 드디어 성공했네요!
content.translation: Nice~! John. You finally did it!

insight.title: 좋았어
insight.usage_tip: Used to celebrate a success or a good move.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "Right on!" I knew I could do it.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "맞아" is a classic. but "바로 그거야" feels a bit more intense.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요! "바로 그거야"는 내가 하고 싶었던 말을 상대방이 딱 했을 때 써요.
content.translation: Right! 'Baro geugeoya' is used when someone says exactly what you wanted to say.

content.speaker: John
content.text: "바로..., 그거야!"
content.translation: That's exactly it!

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 그리고 조금 더 쿨하게 말하고 싶을 땐. "그거지~!" 라고 해보세요.
content.translation: And if you want to say it more coolly. try 'Geugeoji~!'.

insight.title: 그거지
insight.description: 'That's it' or 'That's what I'm talking about'
insight.usage_tip: A trendy way to show strong agreement or satisfaction.

content.speaker: John
content.translation: That's what I'm talking about...,

content.speaker: John
content.text: Is "좋았어" like saying "Okay."

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 음..., "좋았어"는 무언가 결정됐을 때나 잘 됐을 때. "Great!" 또는 "Good!" 이라는 느낌에 더 가까워요.
content.translation: Hmm..., 'Jo-at-seo' is closer to 'Great!' or 'Good!' when something is decided or goes well.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Imagine we are trying to decide what to eat for dinner. and I suggest spicy rice cakes.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 음..., 떡볶이는 어제 먹었잖아요. 다른 거 없어요?
content.translation: Hmm..., we had Tteokbokki yesterday. Anything else?

content.speaker: John
content.text: How about..., Korean fried chicken. with a cold soda.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 와 그거지~! 역시 존 씨는 천재예요!
content.translation: Wow~ 'that's it~!' John. you are a genius!

content.speaker: John
content.text: "Right on!" 치킨에 콜라..., "바로 그거야!"
content.translation: Chicken and cola..., 'that's exactly it!'

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: "좋았어~!" 지금 당장 주문할게요!
content.translation: Nice~! I'll order it right now!

content.speaker: John
content.text: I'm so glad we agreed. "맞아 맞아!"

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 먹는 거 결정할 때가 제일 행복해 보여요.
content.translation: You look happiest when deciding what to eat.

content.speaker: John
content.text: These reactions are so positive and energetic. I love them.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 맞아요. 누군가와 의견이 통했을 때 오늘 배운 표현들을 꼭 써보세요!
content.translation: Right. When you agree with someone. make sure to use the expressions we learned today!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Let's review the five "right on" reactions.

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number one. "맞아~!"
content.translation: Right!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number two. "바로 그거야!"
content.translation: That's exactly it!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number three. "그거지!"
content.translation: That's what I'm talking about!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number four. "좋았어~!"
content.translation: Nice! / Good!

content.speaker: John
content.text: Number five. "정답이야!"
content.translation: You got it! / That's the answer!

insight.title: 정답이야
insight.usage_tip: Used when someone is 100% correct about a fact or a guess.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 여러분도 오늘 "좋았어!" 라고 외칠 만한 즐거운 일들이 많길 바랄게요!
content.translation: I hope you all have many joyful things today that make you shout 'Jo-at-seo!'.

content.speaker: John
content.text: That's all for today. See you in the next episode.

content.speaker: Mina
content.text: 다음에 또 만나요. 안녕~!
content.translation: See you again next time. Bye~!

vocabulary.example: 맞아. 그 말이 사실이야. (Right. that's true.)

vocabulary.meaning: That's it. That's what I'm talking about
vocabulary.example: 우와. 그거지! 정말 멋져요. (Wow. that's it! It's so cool.)
바로 여기가 제가 찾던 곳이에요. (Right here is the place I was looking for.)
좋았어. 이제 시작해 봅시다! (Good. now let's get started!)
정답을 맞혀서 선물을 받았어요. (I got a gift for getting the right answer.)
드디어 실험에 성공했어요. (I finally succeeded in the experiment.)
